results.title: CFPB Highlights Customer Complaints Regarding Reward Programs | Gordon Feinblatt LLC
results.content:
Vague or Hidden Promotional Conditions: Consumers reported discrepancies between promotional materials and the terms and conditions of the reward offers. This has led to consumer frustration concerning alleged "bait and switch" tactics.
 Devalued Rewards: Consumers also complained about the decreased value of rewards caused by factors such as inflation, increased mileage or point requirements, and other changes affecting redemption.
 Customer Service Issues Interfering with Reward Redemption: There were also complaints concerning customer service issues and technical glitches disrupting the redemption process, particularly in collaborative third-party merchant reward programs. [...] Unilateral Revocation of Reward Points: Consumers also raised concerns about the inability to receive reward points when closing accounts and the lack of effective communication from credit card issuers regarding the expiration of certain reward programs. ## CFPB Highlights Customer Complaints Regarding Reward Programs

In a recent industry release, the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) compiled and analyzed consumer complaints received in 2023 regarding credit card reward programs. The CFPB release identified four prevalent issues that consumers consistently raised:

Image 29: Rated 1 out of 5 stars

## I have been a customer of Rewards…
I have been a customer of Rewards Network for over two years, and my experience has been extremely disappointing. They have continued charging my business month after month, but I have seen very little value in return.

Their customer service is one of the worst I’ve dealt with. Every time I call, it’s difficult to get real help, and the representatives are often rude, dismissive, and unwilling to resolve problems. Instead of helping restaurant owners, it feels like they only care about collecting their monthly fees. [...] When I complained that the weekly payment was 2 and even 3 times what they told me the average weekly payment would be, they said they could "slow down" the marketing so payment amounts would come down but it was still above what that average was supposed to be. 

BUT!! HERE'S THE WORST PART!!!

When I finally finished up with the payback they of course asked if I wanted to renew, when I said no, they said ok you have to email us your 30 day notice to quit, because they have to wind down the "rewards" they offer card members. Yet, when I told them I was not renewing because I was selling the business they had said they could do it immediately if I showed them the bill of sale of the restaurant (I'm in the middle of it and hadn't completed the sale).

results.title: Playful Rewards app faces mounting complaints from users
results.content:
# This app says you can win money by playing games. Wisconsin users say they're not getting paid.

Portrait of Tamia Fowlkes

Wisconsin consumers say a company called Playful Rewards LLC promises to pay users for playing games but isn't following through.

Twenty-six consumers have issued complaints about the company to the Better Business Bureau this year.

results.title: Unhappy Customers - How to deal with them using rewards
results.content:
## Become a Pro at Customer Complaint Management

Customer complaint management is essential to maintaining customer relationships and brand loyalty. It is important to have a strategy in place that not only addresses customer complaints but also turns them into opportunities to engage with customers, learn from them, and grow. Reply to a customer complaint and use these tips for effective customer complaint management:

By following these tips, you can make sure that customer complaints are addressed quickly and effectively. Customer satisfaction and loyalty can be maintained by taking the time to listen to their problems and creating a successful strategy for complaint management.

## Dealing with Customer Escalations – For when you messed up bad [...] You messed up with a customer, and now they want to escalate. Despite the fact that it’s difficult, admitting mistakes is necessary if you want to gain your client’s trust. Begin by offering apologies and expressing regret for the mistake. Show your customer that you are empathetic and ready to make things right.

Owning up to your mistakes is always the best thing to do, but sometimes you need a little extra help. You might want to offer something extra to make up for the mistake. This could be a discount, an upgrade, or any gesture that shows your consumer that you care about their satisfaction.

But a discount for the product or service they had an issue with might not be the best idea. Offer them something that will show that you went the extra mile to make up for the mistake. [...] ## How can Huuray help you?

It’s not breaking news that gaining new customers is more costly than keeping your current ones. So making customers happy and dealing with their complaints the right way should be a priority. Whether the product was faulty or they didn’t find the support helpful, some customers will be fuming.

Implement gift cards into your customer service procedures to deal with dissatisfied customers. Huuray makes gifting easy and accessible for businesses all over the globe. And since the best gifts are the ones you choose yourself, give them the freedom to select what they need, when they need it. With Huuray’s Gift Card of Freedom, your customers can choose from more than 5,000 brands. It’s the best way to say  “We’re sorry.”

## Why did my streak reset back to zero?

There are a few reasons why your streak was reset: [...] Note

 This requirement is outlined in the Microsoft Services Agreement.
 Voice over IP (VoIP) phone numbers are not permitted.

## What should I do when my redemption code is invalid?

Ensure you are checking that you are providing the correct code or redemption link. If you continue to experience the problem go to cannot redeem a code.

Note

For a non-Microsoft branded gift card, contact Microsoft Rewards Customer Support.

## Why was my purchase / order cancelled?

Your purchase / order might have been cancelled for several reasons due to using a VoIP phone number, using a VPN or redeeming reward points in multiple regions. To learn more about what might be preventing you from redeeming your points please see Track my Microsoft Rewards orders article. [...] There are a few reasons why your balance is not updating:

 Your Give Mode is on (see Give with Bing FAQ for more details)
 You are logged in to a different account or the browser data is not syncing with the Rewards database data.
 You appear to be attempting to earn points from outside of your current market, which is a violation of program policy.

## Why am I seeing a different balance in my dashboard?

Your Give Mode might be on. See Give with Bing FAQ for more details.

## What is SMS Verification?

We ask for your phone number when you join and, occasionally, when you redeem points for a reward. You'll need a mobile phone or landline number. For help, read Troubleshooting verification code issues
